metadata.title:
FRAUD
W0RK|NGMEN


SUFFER

EMPLOYMENT OFFICE JOBS
NOT AS REPRESENTED, AND
MEN ARE UNABLE TO EARN
MONEY THEY SPENT TO OBJ
TAIN THEM.

pages.raw_text:
Complaints from working men
who are sent by Spokane employ
ment agents to jobs that are not
what they are represented to be
and where conditions prevail that
make it impossible for the men to
stay long enough to earn what they
have expended in reaching the
work continue to come in. The
following is submitted as a version
of such an instance that can be
verified absolutely:


Editor. Press-I would be much
obliged if you would kindly insert
the following for the information
of the general public and working
men, who are fleeced by the en-
ployment agents of Spokane.


| was shipped out by the Scan
dinavian employment agency to
Rockfort, Wash. to work on an ex-
ira gang. paid $3.50 to the agent
as office fee. The slip he gave me
called for $160 per day. When l
arrived at Rockfort | ascertained
that the wages were only $1.44 per
day and was ultimately paid off
at this last rate The number of
men shipped out to this job were
far in excess of the sleeping &C.
commodations provided, so that /,
with many others, was compelled
to sleep on the floor of a boxcar


during the recent spell of severe
weather.


Many were unable to stay with
the job on account of the hard
ships and were compelled to leave
without even recuperating the
money they had paid out to get


work. One man, personal friend


Of mine, was compelled to walk the


whole way back to Spokane, dis


lance of 100 miles, having no


money to pay his fare. Some left


on freight trains.


| was recounting my experience
to other working men at $40 Front
av., where there is an employment


agency, last Saturday night. EN-
ployment Agent Jones, who over
| heard my account, threatened


abusively to have me arrested if


| did not desist, at the same time
threatening others who were only


repeating an article on the same
| question from your paper.
| was recently talking to an en-
ployer of labor and he told that I'M
der existing circumstances a man


cannot obtain work except through
the medium of the employment
agencies.


With reference to recent ar
ticle in a Spokane paper, inserted
| by the Red Cross employment


agent, | wish to fatty contradict it.


! am acquainted with and live
| amongst the men whom he says


will not work. | know this to be
fake. But what they will not dc


is to pay him from So to S. office
| fee, also pay their railroad fare


out to some job where, when they


arrive they are allowed to work
| but a short time and then are OBJ
liged to make room for another
gang, the contractors being in


some scheme with the employment
| agents.


Trusting that you will grant me


the favor of inserting this for the
benefit of the working class, Yours


respectfully, Frank Schultz, 51(


Front av.
